name: Quaderno Tax Automation API
what_it_does: International tax compliance and invoicing platform with REST API — enabling SaaS, digital goods, and subscription businesses to automatically calculate VAT, GST, and sales tax for 100+ countries, generate compliant tax invoices, manage tax-exempt customers, and file tax returns, with native integrations for Stripe, Paddle, PayPal, Shopify, and WooCommerce.
use_cases: Calculating international VAT/GST in real-time via Quaderno API for global SaaS checkout
Generating compliant tax invoices via Quaderno for digital goods sold across EU and UK
Managing B2B VAT exemptions via Quaderno API for reverse charge mechanism compliance
Syncing Stripe transactions to Quaderno for automatic tax invoice generation
Filing OSS (One Stop Shop) VAT returns via Quaderno for EU digital services compliance
not_for: US-only sales tax without international needs (TaxJar or TaxCloud are simpler for US-only)
Physical goods with complex customs and duties requirements (Avalara for complex physical goods)
Large enterprise with dedicated tax teams (Vertex or Avalara for enterprise scale)
best_when: A SaaS, digital goods, or subscription business selling globally needs international VAT/GST compliance — where Quaderno handles EU VAT, UK VAT, Canadian GST, Australian GST, and more with automatic invoice generation and return filing.
avoid_when: You're US-only without international sales, or need complex physical goods duties, or are enterprise-scale with dedicated tax counsel.

auth.notes: API key via HTTP Basic auth (API key as username, password blank). HTTPS required. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. API key from Quaderno account settings. Ruby, PHP, JavaScript, Python client libraries. Webhooks for invoice/payment events. Separate sandbox environment for testing. Idempotency-Key header supported for safe retries.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter €49/month (500 transactions); Pro €99/month (2000 transactions); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Spanish company (Alicante). Founded 2012 by Francesc Llinas. Bootstrapped and profitable. SaaS-first tax platform. Integrations: Stripe, Paddle, Gumroad, PayPal, Shopify, WooCommerce, Fastspring, Chargebee. EU VAT OSS (One Stop Shop) filing. UK VAT post-Brexit. Australian GST, Canadian GST/HST, US sales tax. 100+ countries supported. SaaS-specific features: subscription proration, usage-based billing tax. Tax ID validation (EU VAT number lookup). Reverse charge for B2B. MOSS → OSS migration support. GDPR compliant.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with HTTP Basic auth (API key as username). Ruby/PHP/JS/Python SDKs. Webhooks for events. Sandbox environment. Idempotency-Key header. GDPR compliant. Good developer documentation. International VAT/GST focus for SaaS.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency-Key header supported for POST requests. Tax calculations idempotent for same inputs. Invoice creation idempotent with Idempotency-Key. Read operations fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with API key as username and empty password — not standard Authorization: Bearer pattern
Sandbox and production use different API keys and different base URLs
Use Idempotency-Key header for all POST requests to prevent duplicate invoices
Tax ID validation (EU VAT) has latency — European VIES system lookups can be slow
Webhook signature validation required — verify X-Quaderno-Signature header
Transaction volume limits per plan — monitor usage to avoid overage charges

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth (API key as username). GDPR compliant. EU/Spain data residency. Webhook signature validation. Customer financial data (invoices, tax amounts) is sensitive. No OAuth2 scope granularity. Sandbox isolation. Tax ID data (EU VAT numbers) handled per GDPR.
